description: EcdsaP256Signer::try_sign (lib.rs:207-210) calls p256::ecdsa::Signature::from_slice(&bytes) expecting exactly 64 bytes in fixed-size r||s format (two 32-byte big-endian scalars). This format precondition is only documented in secretx_core::SigningBackend::sign (a different crate), not on EcdsaP256Signer or its try_sign method. A backend author who returns DER-encoded ECDSA output (the other common encoding, typically 70-72 bytes) will get a generic signature::Error with no guidance that the expected format is 64-byte fixed-size r||s, not DER. The Ed25519 and RSA-PSS adapters have no equivalent ambiguity because those algorithms have only one standard byte encoding. Fix: add a doc comment to EcdsaP256Signer or its try_sign explaining the expected byte format and pointing to the SigningBackend::sign docs in secretx-core.
title: EcdsaP256Signer::try_sign byte-format precondition undocumented on the type
